acceptance_criteria:
1. CompiledSignature struct with world, templates, sig_meta
2. RenderedMessages with system and user
3. render_messages entry point
4. render_messages_with_ctx accepting impl Serialize
5. PhantomData for type safety
6. cargo check passes

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: NOTE: Currently signature is at core/signature.rs (single file), not a module directory. Options: (a) create crates/dspy-rs/src/signature/ directory and move/re-export, or (b) put compiled.rs in core/ alongside signature.rs. Recommend option (b) for minimal churn.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Create crates/dspy-rs/src/core/signature/ as a submodule directory. Move existing core/signature.rs content to core/signature/mod.rs, then add compiled.rs alongside it. Update core/mod.rs to use 'mod signature' (directory) instead of single file.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Modern Rust style: Keep core/signature.rs as module root. Create core/signature/ directory and add compiled.rs inside it. In signature.rs add 'mod compiled; pub use compiled::*;' to expose CompiledSignature. No mod.rs needed.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Match existing codebase pattern (see core/lm/): Create core/signature/ directory with mod.rs (move existing signature.rs content there) and compiled.rs. Update core/mod.rs to keep 'pub mod signature;' - it will now resolve to the directory.

title: Implement CompiledSignature struct

acceptance_criteria:
1. All three types compile and are public
2. FieldSpec can be constructed in const/static context (except type_ir call)
3. Types derive Debug, Clone where appropriate
title: Define FieldSpec, ConstraintSpec, and ConstraintKind types
